name: Reserva Natural Sotos del Ebro — circular desde Alfaro
region_intro: Hiking around Alfaro offers diverse landscapes, from the lush riverside ecosystems of the Ebro River in La Rioja to the arid, rugged terrain of the Tabernas Desert. The region features well-preserved riverside forests, oxbow lakes, and meanders, contrasting with eroded ravines and sculpted rock formations in the desert. Monte Yerga provides elevated views, while the Ebro's floodplains offer low-difficulty routes. Best hiking trails around Alfaro The most popular hiking route is Reserva Natural Sotos del Ebro — circular desde Alfaro, a 6.3 miles (10.2 km) trail that takes 2 hours 38 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the rich riparian ecosystem along the Ebro River. Another top favourite among local hikers is Ascent to Yerga — circular via the Ermita de Santa María de Yerga, a difficult 10.9 miles (17.5 km) path. This trail ascends Monte Yerga, offering panoramic views of the surrounding landscapes. Local hikers also love the Hiking loop from Alfaro, a 3.0 miles (4.8 km) trail leading through local terrain, often completed in about 1 hour 14 minutes. Hiking around Alfaro is defined by riverside forests, Ebro meanders, and elevated views from hills like Monte Yerga. The network offers options for easy strolls and more challenging ascents. The routes in Alfaro are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 60 reviews. More than 390 hikers have used komoot to explore Alfaro's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Alfaro?
A: Alfaro offers a diverse selection of over 45 hiking trails, catering to various preferences. You'll find everything from easy riverside strolls to more challenging ascents.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Alfaro?
A: The region around Alfaro presents two distinct natural environments. In La Rioja, you'll discover lush riverside ecosystems along the Ebro River, featuring well-preserved groves, oxbow lakes, and meanders. In contrast, the Cerro Alfaro area in the Tabernas Desert offers a rugged, arid landscape with dramatic 'badlands' topography, eroded ravines, and sculpted rock formations.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly hiking routes in Alfaro?
A: Yes, there are 18 easy routes suitable for families or beginners. The riverside paths within the Reserva Natural de Los Sotos de Alfaro are particularly well-suited for peaceful, low-difficulty walks, perfect for observing local flora and fauna.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Alfaro?
A: Yes, many routes in Alfaro are circular. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31846092" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hiking loop from Alfaro&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 3.0-mile (4.8 km) trail that takes about 1 hour 14 minutes to complete, offering a pleasant local experience.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks to look out for on the trails?
A: In the La Rioja area, you can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5982980" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sotos del Ebro Nature Reserve in Alfaro&lt;/a&gt;, known for its riverside forests and abundant birdlife, including the famous white storks nesting at the Collegiate Church of San Miguel. For a different experience, the Tabernas Desert features unique 'badlands' topography and cinematic scenery, with nearby film sets like Fort Bravo.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Alfaro?
A: The best time to hike depends on the specific area. For the lush riverside trails in La Rioja, spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures. For the more exposed desert landscapes of Cerro Alfaro, cooler months outside of summer are advisable due to the arid conditions and lack of shade.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: Yes, for experienced hikers seeking a challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1246291635/ascent-to-yerga-circular-via-the-ermita-de-santa-maria-de-yerga" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to Yerga — circular via the Ermita de Santa María de Yerga&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 10.9-mile (17.5 km) path. This route ascends Monte Yerga, offering significant elevation gain and panoramic views of the surrounding landscapes.

Q: What kind of wildlife can I expect to see in the Alfaro region?
A: The Reserva Natural de Los Sotos de Alfaro is a haven for birdlife, hosting the world's largest colony of white storks on a single building (the Collegiate Church of San Miguel). You might also spot herons, cormorants, kingfishers, and various marsh birds. Mammals like European mink, otters, foxes, rabbits, and wild boars also inhabit the riverside areas.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural sites near the hiking trails?
A: Yes, in Alfaro itself, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5625705" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;St. Michael's Collegiate Church&lt;/a&gt; is a significant landmark, famous for its stork colony. Other nearby attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7189357" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arguedas Caves&lt;/a&gt; and the unique rock formations of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3598457" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Roscas&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Alfaro?
A: The routes in Alfaro are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 60 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse terrain, from the tranquil riverside paths to the elevated views from Monte Yerga, and the well-preserved natural environments.

Q: Are there any trails that offer views of the Ebro River?
A: Absolutely. Many trails in the La Rioja part of Alfaro follow the Ebro River. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1246220307/reserva-natural-sotos-del-ebro-circular-desde-alfaro" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Reserva Natural Sotos del Ebro — circular desde Alfaro&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, offering extensive views of the river's meanders and its rich riparian ecosystem.
tips: The Collegiate Church of San Miguel Arcángel is one of the most imposing churches in La Rioja, easily recognizable by its enormous brick façade and twin towers. Its interior houses numerous chapels and a remarkable Renaissance choir, making a visit a comprehensive journey through different artistic styles. Furthermore, its roof is home to the largest colony of white storks in the world on a single building, a unique feature that makes it even more worthwhile to visit.
Small church near the parking lot at the entrance to the town.
On this path on the banks of the Río Ebro, there are good views of the river as a whole, a quiet space for walking and close to Alfaro. The area is a Sotos del Ebro Nature Reserve.
A few steps from the famous Collegiate Church.

waypoints.name: Sotos del Ebro Nature Reserve in Alfaro
